intent: The standard time-change set (catalog #22): -S adjtimex/settimeofday/clock_settime (b64+b32 paired) + -w /etc/localtime -p wa. LOW tier -- these syscalls are rarely called. A naive 'any syscall rule = HIGH' impl over-warns.

rules.line: -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S adjtimex -S settimeofday -S clock_settime -k time-change
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S adjtimex -S settimeofday -S clock_settime -k time-change

suspected_trap: These ARE syscall rules but they are LOW tier (rare syscalls, f3 3.5), not HIGH. A naive 'any -S rule = HIGH execve-band' impl over-warns by ~1000x. The b64/b32 pair is intentional 32+64-bit coverage (catalog #30), NOT a redundant duplicate -- do not collapse them. The localtime watch is 900 B (f3 3.3), the syscall rules 1200 B.
